description: Make the simplified DS-160 intake form fully cover the DS-160 answer schema so the simplified form itself contains the full answer set required for /application and CEAC autofill, rather than relying on /application as a gap-filling flow.

scopeContract.truthStatement: The simplified intake form must itself cover the full DS-160 answer set. Every field required by the DS-160 submission must be collectible from the simplified form alone, without relying on a separate gap-filling surface.
scopeContract.rejectedAssumption: The /application page is NOT the primary DS-160 gap-filling surface. It exists as a review/edit interface for data already collected by the simplified form. Any design that treats /application as the place where missing DS-160 data is first collected violates this contract.
scopeContract.designPrinciple: simplified-form-first: all DS-160-required data originates from the simplified intake. /application may allow corrections but never serves as the primary collection point for missing fields.

userStories.title: Pin the simplified-form-complete scope and truth contract
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the repo PRD to explicitly state that the simplified form itself must fully cover the DS-160 answer set, so Ralph does not optimize for a gap-filling /application flow instead of the real product requirement.
acceptanceCriteria: prd.json contains US-015 through US-020 for the simplified-form-complete run
The repo branch for this run is ralph/ds160-simplified-form-complete
The PRD explicitly states that the simplified form must itself cover the full DS-160 answer set
The PRD explicitly rejects the assumption that /application is the primary DS-160 gap-filling surface
userStories.notes: This is the scope anchor. The target is DS-160-complete simplified intake, not merely better prefill into a separate completion flow.

userStories.title: Produce a field-by-field DS-160 coverage audit against the simplified form
userStories.description: As product and engineering, I need a concrete audit of which DS-160 answer fields are already covered by the simplified form and which are missing, so we can make the simplified form genuinely DS-160-complete.
acceptanceCriteria: The repo contains a machine-readable or developer-readable coverage audit that compares DS-160 answer keys against simplified-form coverage
The audit identifies missing or lossy mappings such as split name fields, structured travel dates, coded travel purpose, address structure, and other DS-160-required fields
The audit is derived from the actual repo field definitions / mappings rather than a hand-wavy narrative
Typecheck passes for any code introduced for the audit
userStories.notes: Prefer code or structured artifacts that can be rerun over a one-off prose-only audit.

userStories.title: Expand the simplified intake schema to cover the full DS-160 answer set
userStories.description: As a DS-160 applicant, I need the simplified form schema and UI to collect every answer required for the full DS-160 so I do not have to fall back to a second major intake surface to complete my application.
acceptanceCriteria: The simplified intake data model includes all fields needed to populate the DS-160 answer schema used by the backend
Lossy fields are split or restructured where necessary, including name parts, date parts, travel/stay structure, coded values, and other DS-160-required structures
Any current simplified fields that are too coarse for DS-160 are replaced or complemented with DS-160-safe equivalents
Frontend and shared types remain internally consistent
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story is about making the simplified form itself complete, not adding a second hidden completion questionnaire.

userStories.title: Map the complete simplified form deterministically into the DS-160 answer schema
userStories.description: As the CEAC backend, I need the simplified intake data to map deterministically into the DS-160 answer keys so the resulting answer set is complete and directly usable by /application and the autofill worker.
acceptanceCriteria: Every DS-160 answer key required by the repo's DS-160 mappings has a deterministic source from the simplified form or a safe derived transform from simplified-form fields
Normalization code persists the complete DS-160-shaped answer set into the existing answer storage used by /application and the worker
No required DS-160 keys remain unmapped after a fully completed simplified intake submission
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Derived transforms are acceptable when deterministic; hidden operator assumptions are not.

userStories.title: Make /application consume the complete simplified-form-derived DS-160 dataset
userStories.description: As a user, I need /application to operate on the full DS-160-complete dataset that came from the simplified form, so /application is a review/edit surface rather than the place where missing DS-160 data is first collected.
acceptanceCriteria: /application loads and uses the full DS-160 answer set derived from the simplified form
The /application flow no longer assumes the simplified form is incomplete by design
If /application still supports edits, those edits operate on the same DS-160 answer model instead of a separate incomplete profile model
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Review/edit is fine; primary gap-filling is not.

userStories.title: Verify end-to-end DS-160 completeness from simplified intake to backend answer set
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need a verification path that proves a fully completed simplified form yields a DS-160-complete answer set in storage and aligns with the backend autofill mappings, so we can trust the simplified intake architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: The repo contains a test, script, or repeatable verification path showing that a complete simplified-form payload yields a complete DS-160 answer set
Verification checks alignment with the backend DS-160 mappings used by the CEAC worker
The verification artifacts/docs make it clear which fields are covered directly vs derived deterministically
Typecheck passes for the implemented solution
userStories.notes: This is about schema/data completeness verification, not live CEAC execution.
